name: Univocity
short_description: Univocity is a short cyberpunk visual novel wrapped in neon aesthetics with a lush atmospheric soundtrack, featuring a deep and emotional conversation between two people about taking down a global tech monopoly.
detailed_description:
Univocity is a short, 4K ready, cyberpunk visual novel wrapped in neon aesthetics with a lush atmospheric soundtrack, featuring a deep and emotional conversation between two people about taking down a global tech monopoly and the responsibility that it entails.

Synopsis:

Max, a former engineer who turned fugitive, is headed to the top of tallest tower of Univocity—an urban sprawl owned by Ōṁ, a global tech monopoly—to set off an electromagnetic pulse bomb that will plunge the city into a technological dark age. But their plans are disrupted when they encounter Nina, an Ōṁ employee, which sparks a conversation that makes them question their motives.

Features:

  • Around 30 minutes of interactive gameplay.

  • Deep immersive atmospheric soundtrack by artists such as Patricia Taxxon, Neurotech and NeuroAxis.

  • A self-contained sci-fi story that hints at a larger universe, with themes resonating with currents events. 

  • Tries to find a sweet spot between dystopia and hope.

  • Branching narratives

  • Different endings.

  • Unique backgrounds that use a blend of pixel art and algorithmic art (no AI-generated stuff !)

  • Realistic interactions between characters, based around dilemmas and responsibilities, as well as a tinge of attraction.
